description:
## Goal

Build `guru/preferences.py` — the `UserPreferences` dataclass plus filtering logic shared between vector search and post-retrieval gating.

## File Location

`guru/preferences.py`

## Interface

```python
@dataclass
class UserPreferences:
    mode: str                            # "all" | "whitelist" | "blacklist"
    blacklisted_traditions: list[str]
    blacklisted_texts: list[str]
    whitelisted_traditions: list[str]
    whitelisted_texts: list[str]

    def is_chunk_allowed(self, chunk: RetrievedChunk) -> bool: ...
    def to_vector_filters(self) -> dict: ...
    def active_tradition_summary(self) -> str: ...

    @classmethod
    def allow_all(cls) -> "UserPreferences": ...
    @classmethod
    def from_toml(cls, path: str) -> "UserPreferences": ...
```

## Modes

- `all` — every chunk allowed; vector filter is empty
- `whitelist` — only chunks whose tradition or text is whitelisted
- `blacklist` — every chunk except blacklisted traditions/texts

## Acceptance Criteria

- `to_vector_filters()` produces a clause that the configured vector backend accepts
- `is_chunk_allowed()` agrees with the vector filter (no chunk slips through one but not the other)
- `from_toml` round-trips with a hand-authored `user_preferences.toml`
- `active_tradition_summary()` returns a string suitable for the system prompt's `ACTIVE TRADITIONS:` line

resolution.note: guru/preferences.py: UserPreferences dataclass with mode=all/whitelist/blacklist, is_chunk_allowed(), to_vector_filters() (ChromaDB where-clause), from_toml(), allow_all(), active_tradition_summary().
